body.title: Split credentials by scope: akien/akien.credentials.cfg + Igor-wild-0001/igor.credentials.cfg
body.result: akien.credentials.cfg created with personal API keys; superclaude loads both files (personal wins); T-fix-cmd-next-in-progress-transition + T-fix-granny-role-deferral-test filed for 2 pre-existing test failures
body.intention: I intend that credentials are organized by scope (personal vs instance-specific), so credential loading logic is transparent and Akien's shared keys remain separate from Igor's instance state.
body.description:
Split the monolithic Igor-wild-0001/igor.credentials.cfg into two files by scope: personal credentials (Akien's API keys) at ~/.unseen_university/akien/akien.credentials.cfg, and instance-specific config at ~/.unseen_university/Igor-wild-0001/igor.credentials.cfg. This clarifies ownership and simplifies credential loading logic in superclaude.

**Affected files:** bin/superclaude, ~/../akien/akien.credentials.cfg (new), ~/.unseen_university/Igor-wild-0001/igor.credentials.cfg (modified), devices/inference/sources.py (credential reads remain the same)

**Design rules:** no-live-keys (same as before — organizational only, keys don't move to source); credential-hierarchy (personal > instance-specific in precedence).

**Scope boundary:** IN: split the file, migrate keys to their new locations, update superclaude load order. OUT: changing which keys exist, validating key values, or updating API consumers.

**Completion criteria:** (1) akien/akien.credentials.cfg exists and contains personal API keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, GOOGLE_STUDIO_API_KEY, OLLAMA_API_KEY). (2) Igor-wild-0001/igor.credentials.cfg contains only instance-specific config (IGOR_INSTANCE_ID if any). (3) bin/superclaude loads both in precedence order (personal wins if duplicate keys exist). (4) All existing tests still pass. (5) Manual verification: superclaude reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY successfully from akien.credentials.cfg.
